summary: Parallel review: FAIL (1 critical, 10 warning, 13 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: 🟡 bin/lib/validator-parsers.mjs:121 — `line.trim()` before regex match causes indented TAP subtests to match as top-level failures, producing duplicate findings in nested TAP output. Consider checking indentation level. (Carried forward — appropriate for backlog)

🟡 bin/lib/validator-parsers.mjs:121 — `line.trim()` before TAP regex means indented subtests are matched as top-level failures, producing duplicate findings in nested TAP output. Consider checking indentation depth.
- **Error handling parity** — the prior 🟡 (missing try/catch on `writeGateArtifacts`) was fixed in task-13; all 5 previously flagged yellows are now resolved
🟡 SPEC.md:26 — AC10 specifies a real Jest JUnit reporter integration test; actual tests use inline XML strings. Functionally equivalent but doesn't match spec letter. Backlog item.
🟡 test/parsers.test.mjs:0 — No test for `buildConfigOverride` outputFile path-traversal rejection (gate.mjs:69). Security validation without regression safety net.
🟡 test/worktree.test.mjs:0 — No test for `runGateInline` with `validators.json` config. All config tests go through `cmdGate` subprocess — the inline gate's config path is unwired in tests.
🟡 test/parsers.test.mjs:0 — No test for `processGateOutput` when structured output appears only in stderr. The function combines stdout+stderr (gate.mjs:86), but only stdout is exercised.

🟡 `bin/lib/gate.mjs:69` — `buildConfigOverride` outputFile path-traversal validation has zero direct test coverage. Add unit tests for malicious `outputFile` values (`../../etc/passwd`, absolute paths, backslashes) to prevent regression.

🟡 `bin/lib/gate.mjs:103` — `writeGateArtifacts` does not self-validate `taskId` for path traversal. Both current callers validate correctly, but a future caller that omits `isUnsafeTaskId()` would introduce path traversal via `join(baseDir, "tasks", taskId)`. Add an internal guard clause.
No 🔴 critical findings. Evaluation written to `tasks/task-14/eval-simplicity.md`.
